{
  "judge_id": "JUDGE-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Names the load-bearing concept (DBChat's contribution), shows where the draft drifts from it (by softening or qualifying claims), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.2": "Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., 'produces queries with higher execution accuracy', 'most durable contribution').",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the claim about readability being more than just 'suggestive' without a comparator).",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (DBChat's contribution), shows where the draft drifts from it (by softening or qualifying claims), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.2: Walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., 'produces queries with higher execution accuracy', 'most durable contribution').
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (the claim about readability being more than just 'suggestive' without a comparator).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not.
